edges.description: Loss of membrane-targeted connexins reduces the capacity of keratinocytes to form effective epidermal gap junctions.
Loss of effective epidermal gap-junction communication disrupts the differentiation programs that normally maintain cutaneous homeostasis.
Disturbed epidermal homeostasis produces episodic figurate erythematous patches.
Abnormal keratinization produces persistent hyperkeratotic plaques.
Palmoplantar epidermal keratinization defects manifest as palmoplantar keratoderma.

nodes.description: Abnormal epidermal connexin signaling impairs epidermal differentiation and homeostasis, producing persistent hyperkeratosis and episodic erythematous lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the human connexin gene GJB3 cause erythrokeratodermia variabilis.
evidence.snippet: Our results implicate Cx31 in the pathogenesis of EKV, and provide evidence that intercellular communication mediated by Cx31 is crucial for epidermal differentiation and response to external factors.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant De Novo Mutations in GJA1 Cause Erythrokeratodermia Variabilis et Progressiva, without Features of Oculodentodigital Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: These findings reveal a critical role for Cx43 in epidermal homeostasis, and they provide evidence of organ-specific pathobiology resulting from different mutations within GJA1.

nodes.description: Pathogenic EKV variants disrupt connexin trafficking or membrane localization, preventing normal delivery of epidermal connexins to the cell surface.

evidence.reference_title: Erythrokeratoderma variabilis caused by a recessive mutation in GJB3.
evidence.snippet: Under in vitro conditions, p.V30I prevents Cx31 reaching the cell membrane and taking part in gap-junction formation.

evidence.reference_title: Two de novo GJA1 mutation in two sporadic patients with erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva.
evidence.snippet: GJA1 P283L and T290N mutations lead to Cx43 protein cytoplasmic mislocalization.

nodes.description: Hyperkeratotic plaques are persistent lesions that may be localized or more generalized across the skin.

evidence.snippet: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva (EKVP) is a rare inherited skin disease characterized by fixed hyperkeratotic plaques and transient erythematous patches.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic heterogeneity in erythrokeratodermia variabilis: novel mutations in the connexin gene GJB4 (Cx30.3) and genotype-phenotype correlations.
evidence.snippet: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis is an autosomal dominant genodermatosis characterized by persistent plaque-like or generalized hyperkeratosis and transient red patches of variable size, shape, and location.

nodes.description: Loss of membrane-targeted connexins reduces keratinocyte participation in epidermal gap-junction networks and disrupts intercellular signaling across the epidermis.

evidence.reference_title: Erythrokeratoderma variabilis caused by a recessive mutation in GJB3.
evidence.snippet: Under in vitro conditions, p.V30I prevents Cx31 reaching the cell membrane and taking part in gap-junction formation.

nodes.description: Palmoplantar keratoderma occurs in a substantial subset of affected individuals and broadens the cutaneous disease burden beyond transient erythema and fixed plaques.

evidence.snippet: Approximately 50% of affected individuals develop palmoplantar keratoderma.

evidence.reference_title: Dominant De Novo Mutations in GJA1 Cause Erythrokeratodermia Variabilis et Progressiva, without Features of Oculodentodigital Dysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva (EKVP) is a rare, inherited skin disease that is characterized by transient figurate patches of erythema, localized or generalized scaling, and frequent palmoplantar keratoderma.

nodes.description: Migratory or figurate erythematous patches are a defining feature of EKV and can change location over hours to days.

evidence.snippet: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis et progressiva (EKVP) is a rare inherited skin disease characterized by fixed hyperkeratotic plaques and transient erythematous patches.

evidence.reference_title: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis with adult onset: report of a sporadic case unresponsive to systemic retinoids.
evidence.snippet: Erythrokeratodermia variabilis is a rare autosomal-dominant genodermatosis characterized by migratory and erythematous patches changing over the course of hours to days, and fixed keratotic plaques.
